shortDescription.text: Sensitive data stored in plaintext without encryption
Regular expression vulnerable to catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS)
Cross-site scripting via incorrect output encoding context

message.text: SSN and date-of-birth are stored and retrieved in plaintext. The encryption/decryption helpers in profile-dao.js are commented out, leaving sensitive PII unprotected in the database. An attacker who gains read access to the database (e.g. via NoSQL injection in another endpoint) obtains raw SSN and DOB values.

The getByUserId method returns the full user document including SSN, DOB, bank account, and bank routing numbers directly to the view template without any field filtering or masking. Any user who can trigger profile retrieval receives all sensitive fields in the rendered HTML.

The regex /([0-9]+)+\#/ used to validate bankRouting contains nested quantifiers ('+' inside a group followed by '+'). This causes catastrophic backtracking on crafted input such as '000000000000000000000000000!' — an attacker can send this value to exhaust CPU and cause denial of service.

The user-supplied 'website' field is encoded with ESAPI encodeForHTML(), but the value is also used as a URL in an anchor href attribute. HTML-entity encoding does not prevent javascript: URI schemes or other URL-context attacks. The correct mitigation is encodeForURL() as noted in the source comment.
